content.full_text:
State of California 
Mem randum 
To 
: File 
Date 
:May 11, 1978 
From 
F AIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Ted Prim 
Subject: Disclos 
the Pro Rata Share of Income to a Business Entity 
1. 
If the source of income to a business entity is a 
customer or a client, the name of the source should 
be listed on the client/customer schedule if the pro 
rata share of the official's income meets the relevant 
threshold. 
See 87207(b). 
Once the initial threshold 
has been satisfied, no other reference to the value 
of the income need be set forth on the schedule. 
2. 
If the source of income is not a client (but is 
generated by an asset, for example), the name of the 
source should be listed .on the income disclosure sche-
dule if the official's pro rata share of the income is 
$250 or more. 
The official also must indicate the 
approximate value of his pro rata share of the income 
by checking the appropriate box. 
3. 
If a gift is provided to a business entity and the 
official's pro rata share of the gift is $25 or more, 
the official must report the name of the source and 
the precise value of his pro rata share. 
TP:mfa

sections.question_synthetic: How should officials disclose their pro rata share of income to business entities, including thresholds and reporting requirements for clients, asset-generated income, and gifts?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC provides three disclosure rules: (1) for client/customer income, list the source name once the threshold is met; (2) for non-client income, disclose if the pro rata share is $250+, with approximate value indicated; (3) for gifts to business entities, disclose the source name and precise value if the pro rata share is $25+.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Internal FPPC memorandum format without traditional question/conclusion sections. Content extracted from numbered guidance items. References Government Code Section 87207(b). Author Ted Prim. No formal question posed; guidance appears to be proactive/interpretive rather than responsive to a specific inquiry.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: How should officials disclose their pro rata share of income to business entities, including thresholds and reporting requirements for clients, asset-generated income, and gifts?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC provides three disclosure rules: (1) for client/customer income, list the source name once the threshold is met; (2) for non-client income, disclose if the pro rata share is $250+, with approximate value indicated; (3) for gifts to business entities, disclose the source name and precise value if the pro rata share is $25+.

If the source of income to a business entity is a customer or a client, the name of the source should be listed on the client/customer schedule if the pro rata share of the official's income meets the relevant threshold. See 87207(b). Once the initial threshold has been satisfied, no other reference to the value of the income need be set forth on the schedule. 2. If the source of income is not a client (but is generated by an asset, for example), the name of the source should be listed .on the income disclosure sche- dule if the official's pro rata share of the income is $250 or more. The official also must indicate the approximate value of his pro rata share of the income by checking the appropriate box. 3. If a gift is provided to a business entity and the official's pro rata share of the gift is $25 or more, the official must report the name of the source and the precise value of his pro rata share. TP:mfa
embedding.summary: FPPC memorandum providing guidance on disclosing pro rata shares of income to business entities, with different rules for client/customer sources, asset-generated income, and gifts based on relevant thresholds.
